questions.q: In the Ramayana, Ayodhya is celebrated above all as the birthplace of which divine prince?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya is renowned in the Ramayana as the birthplace of Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: On the banks of which river does the Ramayana place the ancient city of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana situates Ayodhya on the banks of the river Sarayu.

questions.q: Of which kingdom was Ayodhya the capital in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was the capital of the kingdom of Kosala in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Rama, the prince of Ayodhya, is revered as an avatar of which of the great Hindu deities?
questions.explanation: Rama of Ayodhya is honored as the seventh avatar of Vishnu, the preserver.

questions.q: Which famed royal dynasty is described in the Ramayana as ruling from Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was ruled by the Ikshvaku dynasty, the solar line into which Rama was born.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the aged king of Ayodhya and the father of Rama?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha was the ruler of Ayodhya and the father of Rama.

questions.q: The ideal of just and harmonious governance associated with Rama's reign in Ayodhya is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Rama's reign in Ayodhya is upheld as 'Rama Rajya,' the paragon of an ideal and righteous realm.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the devoted wife of Rama who shares his exile from Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sita, the consort of Rama, accompanies him when he leaves Ayodhya for the forest.

questions.q: Which of these is Ayodhya traditionally counted among in Hindu sacred geography?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya is reckoned one of the seven sacred cities (Sapta Puri) of pilgrimage in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: After his long exile and the war against Ravana, Rama returns to Ayodhya for what great event?
questions.explanation: Rama returns to Ayodhya to be crowned king, an event central to the city's significance.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, at whose insistence is Rama sent into exile from Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi, prompted by her maid Manthara, demands Rama's exile so her own son Bharata may be crowned.

questions.q: For how many years is Rama exiled from Ayodhya according to the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama is sent into exile from Ayodhya for fourteen years.

questions.q: Which brother of Rama governs the kingdom of Ayodhya as regent during Rama's exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata rules Ayodhya as regent during the exile, placing Rama's sandals upon the throne in his stead.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Bharata place upon the throne of Ayodhya to signify that Rama is the rightful king during his absence?
questions.explanation: Bharata enthrones Rama's sandals (paduka) and rules as a servant of the absent king.

questions.q: From which nearby village does Bharata govern Kosala rather than dwell in the palace at Ayodhya during Rama's exile?
questions.explanation: Bharata governs from Nandigrama, near Ayodhya, living austerely until Rama's return.

questions.q: The grief of which king of Ayodhya at parting from Rama leads to his death during the prince's exile, as told in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya dies of grief after Rama departs for exile.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the elder son Rama is born in Ayodhya to King Dasharatha and which of his queens?
questions.explanation: Rama is born to Queen Kaushalya, the eldest queen of Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: The lighting of lamps to welcome Rama back to Ayodhya is widely associated with the origin of which festival?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the people of Ayodhya lit rows of lamps to welcome Rama home, a story linked to the festival of Diwali.

questions.q: Which sage is traditionally honored as the author of the Sanskrit epic Ramayana, which recounts the story of Ayodhya and Rama?
questions.explanation: The sage Valmiki is revered as the author of the Sanskrit Ramayana, the foremost text on Ayodhya and Rama.

questions.q: Which revered sage serves as the royal preceptor (kulaguru) of the Ikshvaku kings of Ayodhya in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The sage Vasishtha is the family preceptor of the royal house of Ayodhya in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the great fire-sacrifice King Dasharatha of Ayodhya performs to obtain sons is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha performs the Putrakameshti yajna at Ayodhya, after which his queens bear four sons.

questions.q: Counting Rama, how many sons are born to King Dasharatha of Ayodhya in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha has four sons: Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: According to tradition, which ancient lawgiver and progenitor is said to have founded the city of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Manu, the progenitor of the Ikshvaku line, with the founding of Ayodhya.

questions.q: The Sanskrit name 'Ayodhya' is traditionally understood to carry which meaning?
questions.explanation: The name Ayodhya is traditionally interpreted as 'that which cannot be warred against' or the unconquerable city.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the kanda (book) describing court intrigue and Rama's banishment from Ayodhya is named after the city. What is it called?
questions.explanation: The Ayodhya Kanda is the second book of Valmiki's Ramayana, centered on events in the city before Rama's exile.

questions.q: Which legendary Ikshvaku king of Ayodhya is famed in the Puranas for his austerities that brought the river Ganga down from heaven?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha, a king of the Ikshvaku line of Ayodhya, performed great penance to bring the Ganga to earth.

questions.q: Which truthful king of Ayodhya's Ikshvaku line is renowned in tradition for never abandoning truth despite the loss of his kingdom and family?
questions.explanation: King Harishchandra of the solar dynasty of Ayodhya is celebrated for his unwavering devotion to truth.

questions.q: From which illustrious ancestor of the Ayodhya line does Rama derive the epithet 'Raghava' and the dynastic name Raghuvamsha?
questions.explanation: The name Raghava and the Raghuvamsha derive from King Raghu, a celebrated forebear of Rama at Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda of the Ramayana, where are Rama's twin sons Lava and Kusha, born after Sita leaves Ayodhya, raised?
options: In the court of King Janaka
At the hermitage of the sage Valmiki
questions.explanation: Lava and Kusha are born and raised at the hermitage of the sage Valmiki after Sita departs Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which Awadhi-language retelling of Rama's story, composed by the saint-poet Tulsidas, helped spread the devotion centered on Ayodhya and Rama across North India?
questions.explanation: Tulsidas's Ramcharitmanas, composed in Awadhi, greatly spread the devotional tradition of Rama and Ayodhya in North India.
